source_jobs.title: Product Manager - Security Analytics, Threat Intelligence & AI (Redwood City, CA)
source_jobs.description:
Company Description:
 
Anomali is headquartered in Silicon Valley and is the Leading AI-Powered Security Operations Platform that is modernizing security operations. Anomali delivers the first Intelligence-Native Agentic SOC Platform, unifying a fully-featured-security data lake, threat intelligence, and agentic AI into a single modern experience. Today, we operate in a space where organizations will be left behind if they don’t begin to think and operate differently. Automating important tasks will free up time for operators to do more advanced IT and security work.
Learn more at www.anomali.com
 
Job Description:
Anomali is building the next generation of AI-driven security operations, combining threat intelligence, security analytics, and autonomous workflows to help security teams detect, investigate, and respond faster.
We are looking for a Product Manager to join our growing product team and work across core areas of our platform, including threat intelligence, security analytics, and AI. This is a horizontal, cross-functional role for a PM who wants to build deep technical and domain expertise across the security operations stack while owning meaningful product areas end-to-end.
You will own discrete product areas and features, partner closely with engineering, design, data, field, and go-to-market teams, and execute within a strategic framework set by Product Leadership. This is not a siloed role. Success requires strong alignment with company direction and the ability to translate strategy into clear, practical execution.
A specific focus area for this position will be on On-Prem deployments.
 
Key Responsibilities:
 
Own the full product lifecycle for assigned features and capabilities, from discovery and requirements through launch, adoption, and iteration
Translate ambiguous problems into clear PRDs, user stories, and acceptance criteria that enable high-quality engineering execution
Engage directly with customers and prospects to uncover real needs, validate solutions, and continuously refine the product
Partner closely with engineering, design, data, intelligence, analytics, AI, and field teams to ensure product decisions are grounded in platform capabilities and customer outcomes
Align roadmap priorities with Product Leadership and company strategy while shaping feature-level direction and execution
Support go-to-market efforts through competitive analysis, sales enablement, and customer-facing documentation
Communicate progress, risks, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to stakeholders across product, engineering, go-to-market, and leadership teams
Contribute to Anomali’s evolving AI roadmap, helping define how autonomous workflows and agentic capabilities are incorporated into the platform
 
Qualifications
Required Skills/Experience:
You are a product manager who can take complex, ambiguous problems and turn them into shipped capabilities with clear customer and business impact.  
 
3+ years of product management experience, or equivalent experience in a technical or product-adjacent role such as engineering, TPM, or solutions architecture with relevant technologies including on-prem deployments, hardware, clustering and Linux based OS
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or other relevant discipline required.  Master's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data, MBA or other relevant advanced education or certifications highly desirable
Demonstrated ability to translate ambiguity into structured requirements with clear acceptance criteria
Comfort working across technical and business audiences; you can collaborate deeply with engineers and communicate effectively with senior security leaders, including CISOs
Ability to operate effectively in a matrixed, cross-functional environment, taking strategic direction from Product Leadership while owning execution at the feature and product-area level
Familiarity with agile development practices and modern PM tooling such as Jira, Confluence, or sim

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative - MSSPs and Channel (US Remote)
source_jobs.description:
Company Description 
Anomali is headquartered in Silicon Valley and is the Leading AI-Powered Security Operations Platform that is modernizing security operations. At the center of it is an omnipresent, intelligent, and multilingual Anomali Copilot that automates important tasks and empowers your team to deliver the requisite risk insights to management and the board in seconds. The Anomali Copilot navigates a proprietary cloud-native security data lake that consolidates legacy attempts at visibility and provides first-in-market speed, scale, and performance while reducing the cost of security analytics. Anomali combines ETL, SIEM, XDR, SOAR, and the largest repository of global intelligence in one efficient platform. Protect and drive your business with better productivity and talent retention. 
 
Do more with less. Be Different. Be the Anomali. 
Learn more at http://www.anomali.com.
 
Job Description
Anomali is looking for an energetic, self-driven Sales Development Representative to join our team! You will help lead the prospecting and qualifying efforts in-region, tied specifically to the Americas sales efforts. As a Sales Development Representative at Anomali, you will be a key contributor to the company’s growth and you will gain great insight into how the full sales-cycle works, exposure to high profile customers, a strong technical grounding in our product, and hands-on mentorship and training.
 
Responsibilities 
o Strategic prospecting into both large enterprise & mid-market accounts 
o Design and execute multi-channel "sequences" (Phone, Email, LinkedIn) that prioritize relevance over volume. 
o Engage the C-Suite: Master the art of the "Executive Bridge"—qualifying high-level stakeholders at Global 2000 companies. 
o Familiarity with MSSPs (Managed Security Service Providers)/ Channel partner ecosystems required
o Complete a Threat Intelligence and Security concepts sales training program, and receive advanced training throughout your career 
o Develop and maintain a strong pipeline via phone, email & social selling campaigns 
o Stay on pulse with Anomali’s technology, industry trends and competition 
o Capture your work in Salesforce.com CRM and Gong 
 
Required Skills/Experience
o Prior B2B, Enterprise SaaS SDR/BDR experience required. Relevant cybersecurity product experience is also required.
o Prior Sales Development experience with MSSPs and/or cybersecurity channel partners
o Knowledge of enterprise business organizational structures and buying influencers.
o Career: passion for sales and technology - desire to ‘grow’ your sales career with a top-notch cybersecurity vendor.
o Coachability: able to listen and take training/feedback with a positive approach.
o Relationships: a team player and ability to “connect” with all individuals of a business including the ‘C-Suite’.
o Personality: outgoing, highly energetic, enthusiastic and a risk-taker who thrives in dynamic work climates.
o Work ethic: self-motivated and strong desire to learn concepts about your new role and industry in a fast-paced manner.
o Adaptability: open and eager to understand bleeding edge information, security concepts and approaches in a rapidly-changing environment.
o Communication style: clear, concise and effective communicator.
o Education Level: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience (3yrs relevant professional experience).
o Prior experience with sales tools including SDFC, Marketo, Hubspot, LI Sales Navigator, ZoomInfo, Outreach, GongEngage, or comparable tools required
o Plus: structured sales training and/or Salesforce.com experience
o This position is not eligible for employment visa sponsorship. The successful candidate must not now, or in the future, require visa sponsorship to work in the US.

o Desire to learn new technology, ramp quickly and grow with Anomali.

source_jobs.title: Senior Account Manager - KSA
source_jobs.description:
Executive Overview:
 Anomali is headquartered in Silicon Valley and is the Leading AI-Powered Security Operations Platform that is modernizing security operations. At the center of it is an omnipresent, intelligent, and multilingual Anomali Copilot that automates key workflows and empowers your team to deliver critical threat insights to leadership in seconds.
 
Anomali unifies ETL, SIEM, XDR, SOAR, and the world’s largest repository of global intelligence into a single, cloud-native platform that improves detection, speeds investigations, and reduces costs at scale.
 
Do more with less. Be Different. Be the Anomali.
Learn more at www.anomali.com
 
Our team is passionate, innovative and creative with a smart, healthy and energetic winning sales culture without egos or politics — a group of professionals who share a common vision and passion to build the best and most trusted cybersecurity analytics company in the world. This is an important role in a rapidly expanding region of the company.
 
Objective
We are currently looking for talented and passionate Account Manager for KSA to join our EMEA enterprise sales team and develop and lead the region. We are known for having an upbeat and healthy, dynamic environment with a culture focused on career growth, success and winning! Our business model allows our sales teams to manage the full life cycle of our customer engagement. We do not cap commissions!
 
The primary responsibility is for the go to market strategy for the region and grow the revenue of Anomali’s security solutions into enterprise and government organizations within an eco-system of channel and technology partners.
 
Location – Saudi Arabia, Ideally located in Riyadh.
 
Job Description
 
Responsibilities
 
o Develop a regional territory go-to-market plan, incorporating a sales, marketing and channel plan with execution milestones.
o As an enterprise sales role, the Account Manager for KSA will work in close partnership with an established and very experienced Sales Engineer, who is already based in the region to support this role.
o Actively participate in all areas of the regional business. Build and drive the sales motion for the region establishing and maintaining all client and channel relationships.
o Above all else, be accountable for results to exceed sales targets for the region on a regular basis.
o Represent Anomali as the main spokesperson and figurehead for the region. Evangelize to the market and partners. Host key industry and Anomali events for the region.
o Work closely with our strategic customers to be their advocate in Anomali for additional needs, identifying new business opportunities, and ensuring smooth and on-time renewals.
o Work with the customer success team to develop strong regional Anomali customer advocates who can be an important part of our sales motion / GTM and create up-sell.
o Travel as required across the region either working directly with end-users or with channel / eco-system partners.
o Build and communicate consistent, accurate forecast information weekly, monthly and quarterly, giving the VP, MEA and SVP International excellent visibility into the region’s pipeline.
o Develop an Anomali eco-system within the region with key decision makers, influencers and partners. Find and develop ‘lighthouse accounts’ for the region.
o Work closely with the channel team to evolve the partner channel both in terms of select SIs, VARs, distributors and technology partners to drive maximum value and incremental pipeline growth for the region.
 
Accountabilities & Performance Measures
 
o Exceeds assigned sales quotas in designated region.
o Responsible for the development and performance of all sales activities in assigned markets.
o Provides accurate reporting and forecasting for the region.
o Establishes plans and strategies to expand the customer base in the region.
 
Organizational Alignment
 
o Reports to Samer Jadallah, VP MEA
o Works closely in partnership with the aligned regional

source_jobs.title: Territory Account Executive with SIEM / Security Analytics (US NY/NJ)
source_jobs.description:
Company Description:
 
Anomali is headquartered in Silicon Valley and is the Leading AI-Powered Security Operations Platform that is modernizing security operations. At the center of it is an omnipresent, intelligent, and multilingual Anomali Copilot that automates important tasks and empowers your team to deliver the requisite risk insights to management and the board in seconds. The Anomali Copilot navigates a proprietary cloud-native security data lake that consolidates legacy attempts at visibility and provides first-in-market speed, scale, and performance while reducing the cost of security analytics. Anomali combines ETL, SIEM, XDR, SOAR, and the largest repository of global intelligence in one efficient platform. Protect and drive your business with better productivity and talent retention. 
 
Do more with less. Be Different. Be the Anomali.
 
Learn more at http://www.anomali.com.
 
 
Job Description:
 
We are currently looking for talented and passionate individuals to join our enterprise sales team.  Anomali has experienced explosive growth over the past 18 months and are looking to expand our team immediately. We are known for having an upbeat and dynamic environment with a culture focused on career growth, success and winning!  Our business model allows our Territory Account Executives to manage the full life cycle of our customer engagement.
 
Additionally, we have an award winning and proven training program to get individuals ramp up quickly and simultaneously allowing them to move forward in their careers, both through management and direct promotions. 
 
We do not cap commissions!
 
Territory: multi-state region including NY and NJ.  Candidates must reside within their assigned territory.
 
Qualifications
 
Required Skills/Experience:
o Minimum of 2 years in B2B SaaS cybersecurity sales/sales development experience with mid-market/enterprise businesses. 
o A minimum of 18 months mid-market or enterprise closing experience is required
o Full life cycle solution-selling. Experience with relevant SaaS Security or data security products is required.
o Ability to “connect” with all individuals at all levels, C-Suite to Analyst.
o Desire to learn new technology, ramp quickly and grow with Anomali.
o Record of over achievement in Enterprise prospecting and sales.
o Personal selling experience into various verticals.
o Executive level relationships with Key Accounts.
o High energy, enthusiastic and effective communicator.
o Ability to understand information security concepts and approaches.
o Experience in SIEM, Big Data, Cloud or Threat Intelligence product sales is required.
o Strong work ethic, ability to adapt to rapidly-changing environments.  Self Starter.
o Proven ability to generate pipeline and close business.
o Residing within the territory/specified location. (NY/NJ)
o Bringing a network of contacts and partner relationships within the assigned territory.
o Salesforce experience preferred.
o Open to travel up to 50%
o This position is not eligible for employment visa sponsorship. The successful candidate must not now, or in the future, require visa sponsorship to work in the US.

source_jobs.title: UX Designer (Belfast)
source_jobs.description:
Company Description
Anomali, headquartered in Silicon Valley, delivers the first Intelligence-Native Agentic SOC Platform — unifying a security data lake, the world's largest IOC repository, threat intelligence, and agentic AI into a single modern experience. The platform accelerates detection, investigation, and response, delivering earlier insights, faster action, and scalable modernization across any environment.
Whether augmenting existing tools or delivering complete SOC capabilities end-to-end, Anomali empowers security teams to operate faster, smarter, and with confidence.
Beyond Detecting. Start Deciding. Start Acting.
Learn more at www.anomali.com
 
Responsibilities
 
We're looking for a mid-level UX Designer who blends strong research craft with excellent design skills. You'll spend your time uncovering what our users — CTI analysts, threat hunters, and SOC teams — actually need, then translating those insights into an intuitive experience for our enterprise platform. You'll also help shape how AI shows up in our work — both in the products our analysts use and in how our team designs.
What You'll Do

Design & Product Partnership

Work closely with product management to turn research insights into clear, well-scoped product requirements.

Translate insights into workflows and interactive prototypes.

Help PMs pressure-test ideas early — using prototypes and research to validate before engineering invests.

Rapidly iterate on concepts using AI-assisted prototyping tools.

Partner closely with engineering to ensure design intent carries through to implementation.

Contribute to and apply our evolving design system.

Research

Plan and run end-to-end studies: user interviews, usability tests, surveys.

Build and maintain ongoing customer feedback loops with our enterprise users.

Synthesize qualitative and quantitative findings into clear, actionable insights.

Monitor and report on product usage analytics, surfacing trends and anomalies that shape research priorities.

Partner with product managers and engineers to identify the right questions to ask at each stage of the product lifecycle.
 
Present findings in compelling ways — written reports, readouts, journey maps, personas.
Advocate for the user across the org and help shape roadmap decisions with evidence.
 
Required Skills/Experience
3+ years combined experience across UX research and design.
Strong skills in interaction design and information architecture, especially for dense, data-heavy interfaces.
Experience designing complex enterprise or B2B software — data-dense workflows, power users, technical domains.
Demonstrated strength in qualitative research methods: customer/user interviews, usability studies, contextual inquiry.
Experience monitoring and reporting on product analytics.
Proficiency in Figma for ideation and design system contribution.
Hands-on experience using AI tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf) for rapid prototyping and exploration.
Excellent written and verbal communication; able to tell a clear, compelling story with research and design work.
A portfolio showing both design and research outputs is required.
Nice to Have
Experience designing for cybersecurity, threat intelligence, SOC operations, or related security domains.
Experience integrating research into agile product development cycles.
BA/BS in HCI, Psychology, Human Factors, Cognitive Science, Design, or related field (or equivalent practical experience).
Benefits:
o Competitive Salary
 
Medical
o Private Healthcare Plan
o Dental Plan
o Optical Plan
 
 Work-Life Balance
o Paid Public Holidays
o Accrued Paid Time Off – 25 days

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Americas Channels & Alliances (US Remote)
source_jobs.description:
Company Description
Anomali, headquartered in Silicon Valley, delivers the first Intelligence-Native Agentic SOC Platform — unifying a security data lake, the world's largest IOC repository, threat intelligence, and agentic AI into a single modern experience. The platform accelerates detection, investigation, and response, delivering earlier insights, faster action, and scalable modernization across any environment.
Whether augmenting existing tools or delivering complete SOC capabilities end-to-end, Anomali empowers security teams to operate faster, smarter, and with confidence.
Beyond Detecting. Start Deciding. Start Acting.
Learn more at www.anomali.com
 
Position Description
Anomali is seeking a Vice President, Americas Channels & Alliances to lead and scale our indirect go-to-market ecosystem across North America. Reporting directly to the Chief Commercial Officer, this leader will be responsible for developing and executing a comprehensive channel and alliance strategy.  Anomali delivers the first Intelligence-Native Agentic SOC Platform — unifying a security data lake, the world's largest IOC repository, threat intelligence, and agentic AI into a single modern experience. The platform accelerates detection, investigation, and response, delivering earlier insights, faster action, and scalable modernization across any environment.
Whether augmenting existing tools or delivering complete SOC capabilities end-to-end, Anomali empowers security teams to operate faster, smarter, and with confidence.
Beyond Detecting. Start Deciding. Start Acting.
Role Overview
The Vice President will build and lead a high-performing partner organization encompassing distributors, value-added resellers (VARs), managed security service providers (MSSPs), systems integrators, cloud marketplace partners, hyperscalers, and strategic technology alliances. This individual will serve as a key member of the Commercial Leadership Team and will work cross-functionally to drive partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue while creating scalable routes to market for Anomali's platform and solutions.
This is a highly visible leadership role requiring a proven track record of building partner ecosystems, driving revenue through indirect channels, and developing executive-level relationships across the cybersecurity market. The successful candidate will help shape Anomali's ecosystem strategy to accelerate customer adoption of AI-powered security operations and next-generation cyber defense capabilities.

Key Responsibilities
Channel Strategy & Revenue Growth
Develop and execute the Americas channel and alliance strategy aligned with Anomali's corporate growth objectives.
Build a scalable and predictable partner business that contributes significantly to pipeline generation, bookings, and revenue growth.
Establish annual business plans, revenue targets, and growth initiatives across all partner segments.
Identify and prioritize strategic partnerships that accelerate customer acquisition, market expansion, and competitive differentiation.
Own partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue objectives across the Americas.
Partner Ecosystem Development
Recruit, develop, and grow a high-performing ecosystem of distributors, VARs, MSSPs, systems integrators, cloud partners, and technology alliances.
Establish executive-level relationships with key partners and act as Anomali's senior sponsor within strategic accounts.
Create and maintain a disciplined operating cadence including business reviews, joint planning sessions, pipeline reviews, and executive engagement.
Drive partner activation, productivity, and long-term growth through structured engagement and measurable performance metrics.
Go-to-Market Execution
Partner closely with Sales leadership to drive joint account planning, pipeline development, and co-sell motions.
Develop repeatable partner-led and partner-assisted sales motions that increase market coverage and accelerate deal velocity.
Collaborate wit

source_jobs.title: Vice President, Global Sales Engineering (US Remote)
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW:
Anomali is headquartered in Silicon Valley and is the Leading AI-Powered Security Operations Platform that is modernizing security operations. At the center of it is an omnipresent, intelligent, and multilingual Anomali Copilot that automates important tasks and empowers your team to deliver the requisite risk insights to management and the board in seconds. The Anomali Copilot navigates a proprietary cloud-native security data lake that consolidates legacy attempts at visibility and provides first-in-market speed, scale, and performance while reducing the cost of security analytics. Anomali combines ETL, SIEM, XDR, SOAR, and the largest repository of global intelligence in one efficient platform. Protect and drive your business with better productivity and talent retention. 

Do more with less. Be Different. Be the Anomali.
Learn more at http://www.anomali.com.

POSITION SUMMARY
The Vice President, Global Solutions Engineering is responsible for developing and leading Anomali’s Sales Engineering team, ensuring they provide exemplary pre-sales support to the sales team focusing on territory and enterprise accounts. Anomali’s Sales Engineers assist in driving new business from prospective and existing customer accounts within their respective assigned territory, and meeting and/or exceeding all sales quotas to achieve revenue goals. This position will organize, plan, direct, and coordinate all aspects of the technical pre-sales engagement experience for Anomali globally. This leader will also be responsible for working cross-functionally with sales and product management to align customer requirements and accelerate growth.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 
Reporting to the Chief Revenue Officer, this executive will be a strategic member of the leadership team with a strong background in cybersecurity, and a combination of exceptional technology and communication skills to ensure customer adoption of Anomali’s solutions. The placed candidate will: 

·        Develop and maintain a clear vision for the Sales Engineering team while bringing accountability, energy, a results-orientation, and a growth mentality to the organization. 
·        Lead the Regional Sales Engineers Managers in developing their teams and exceeding their performance targets. 
·        Align closely with sales to develop success plans and engagement strategies to drive increased ARR at existing and new customers. Attend key customer meetings to present Anomali solutions.
·        Provide exemplary pre-sales technical expertise through technical and product demonstrations and train the global Sales Engineer organization to perform at the highest level. 
·        Work closely with Anomali leadership team on sales strategy, product direction and solution positioning. 
·        Act as a business resource to the customer/prospect and provide “industry expert” executive level selling practices with consistent communication and on-going technical consultation. 
·        Proactively drive Strategic and Enterprise account opportunities within key territory accounts.
·        Leverage extensive market knowledge to be a trusted advisor to the customer to shorten the sales cycle. 
·        Work in conjunction with Sales, Marketing, and Product Managers. The Sales Engineer (SE) is the technical bridge between Anomali’s Enterprise Business Representatives (demand generation) and their pre-sales customers.
·        Host continuous sales enablement for market, product, and competitive knowledge to the field sales engineering team. 
·        Evaluate and develop ways to streamline the proof of value sales cycle with direct and channel partners.
·        Provide hands-on experience to help build solution demonstrations that cater to the customers business use cases. 
·        Participate in regional industry events/organizations as an industry thought leader.
 
 
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE/QUALIFICATIONS 
The Vice President, Global Sales Engineering will be an outstanding

notes: Anomali is a security operations platform with a strong focus on AI and automation. The company has a mix of technical and non-technical roles, but the majority of the roles are in sales and account management.
